summary: CC-0114 moves the K-ORC bootstrap clouds.yaml seed from a deploy-infra shell script into the c5c3-operator via three new reconcileKORC steps (seedBootstrapCloudsYAML, EnsurePushSecret, ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret). From a code-quality/architecture standpoint the change is clean: it reuses buildPasswordCloudsYAML and appCredCloudsYAMLKey (DRY), all sub-resource names derive from cp.Name (multi-instance safe), the three steps are idempotent/no-churn on steady state (proven by tests including IdempotentNoChurn), the seed is correctly write-if-empty with a re-mint repopulate path, and the new code matches the sibling CreateOrUpdate + inline-Reason-string style already used by ensureAdminPasswordCloud/ensureAppCredentialSecret. The placement DECISION comment is sound (it correctly identifies the issue's two stated constraints as mutually inconsistent given the real call order ensureKORCAdminImports@423 before ensureAppCredentialSecret@450, and intra-pass ordering does not affect async K-ORC convergence), and the esov1 (ExternalSecret, GA) vs esov1alpha1 (PushSecret) split is intentional and documented. No defects found in changed files.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests cover seedBootstrapCloudsYAML write-if-empty, no-overwrite-minted, re-mint repopulate
Unit tests cover ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret shape, owner ref, per-CR remote key, idempotent no-churn
Reworked deadlock test asserts operator now creates PushSecret + ExternalSecret on fresh cluster
Integration test asserts operator creates the ES (shape/owner) instead of pre-creating it
Rendered clouds.yaml document parse + endpoint_type-not-interface tests retained for the seed path

code_quality_checklist.item: buildPasswordCloudsYAML reused by seed (no duplicated YAML template)
appCredCloudsYAMLKey constant reused for Secret key, ES SecretKey, and RemoteRef Property
New code matches sibling CreateOrUpdate + inline-Reason-string style (no gratuitous new pattern)
Error wrapping with %w and contextual messages on every new path
Placement DECISION and esov1-vs-esov1alpha1 rationale documented in comments

security_checklist.item: Password embedded via buildPasswordCloudsYAML (%q) and only ever materialised into a Secret / pushed via ESO — no host process-arg exposure (improvement over deleted shell seed)
SetControllerReference set on seeded Secret and created ExternalSecret for GC and ownership
Write-if-empty guard prevents clobbering a minted credential-based clouds.yaml

architecture_checklist.item: Placement after ensureAppCredentialSecret, before re-mint decision — sound given real call order and async K-ORC retry
ExternalSecret created in childNamespace(cp) co-located with K-ORC CRs (CloudCredentialsRef resolved in resource namespace)
ExternalSecret name derives from Spec CloudCredentialsRef.SecretName with korcCloudsYamlSecretName fallback
esov1 (GA ExternalSecret) vs esov1alpha1 (PushSecret) split correct and registered in main.go scheme
Static single-identity ESO manifest retired; orc-system copy retained; shell seed fully removed

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated clouds.yaml builder — single buildPasswordCloudsYAML
Double CreateOrUpdate over the same Secret is justified by SRP (value vs clouds.yaml) + distinct inputs; not premature consolidation

fail_fast_checklist.item: Each new step returns ctrl.Result{} + err with a distinct condition Reason on failure
No swallowed errors in the three new steps

defensive_checklist.item: Empty SecretName falls back to korcCloudsYamlSecretName constant
Steady-state passes are no-ops (write-if-empty + spec-diff CreateOrUpdate) — no churn / no consumer wakeups

suggested_improvements: OPTIONAL (no action required): seedBootstrapCloudsYAML and ensureAppCredentialSecret each run a separate Get+Update CreateOrUpdate over the same {cp.Name}-admin-app-credential Secret on the fresh-cluster path. This is correct and SRP-clean as written (distinct keys, distinct inputs, distinct failure Reasons), and is a no-op on steady state. If a future change ever wants to shave one round-trip, the two could be merged into a single CreateOrUpdate mutator — but doing so would require threading password into ensureAppCredentialSecret and merging two responsibilities, so the current separation is the better trade-off and should be kept unless a measured hot-path cost appears.

OPTIONAL (repo-wide, out of scope): the external-feedback 'extract repeated condition strings into typed constants' pattern is not applied here, but the new inline Reason strings correctly match the existing convention throughout reconcile_korc.go (SecretError, AdminImportError, KORCCRDNotInstalled, ...). Extracting Reasons into typed constants would be a separate repo-wide cleanup, not a CC-0114 concern.
detected_patterns: controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate + SetControllerReference for owned sub-resources (matches sibling ensureAdminPasswordCloud/ensureAppCredentialSecret)
write-if-empty idempotency guard for seeded Secret keys
per-CR resource naming derived from cp.Name / Spec refs for multi-instance safety
esov1 GA ExternalSecret vs esov1alpha1 PushSecret API-version split
